string.text: The J.C. Higgins 583 shotgun, manufactured for Sears, Roebuck, and Co., was a reliable and versatile bolt-action firearm of the mid-20th century. With a classic design featuring a wooden stock and forearm, it offered a comfortable grip for users. Chambered for 12 gauge, the shotgun boasted a typical barrel length of around 28 inches, striking a balance between maneuverability and effective range. Its bolt-action mechanism allowed for the manual cycling of rounds from the tubular magazine, making it durable and capable of handling various ammunition types. Known for its solid construction and performance, the J.C. Higgins 583 shotgun was a widely recognized firearm that found utility in hunting and self-defense applications. \n \n
